skill.name: tdd-workflows-tdd-red
skill.description: Generate failing tests for the TDD red phase to define expected behavior and edge cases.
skill.summary: Generate failing tests for the TDD red phase to define expected behavior and edge cases.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick examples, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance-like words in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives in framework guidance and sample test text, with no executable commands, credential access, network behavior, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Framework References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 71, 72, 73, 82, 83, and 86 contain inline Markdown references to testing APIs and libraries, not Ruby shell backtick execution. The text is guidance for generating tests and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown prose and lists framework names such as jest.fn(), fast-check, t.Parallel(), and let. No executable script or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3 and 134 were flagged as weak cryptography, but line 3 is the skill description and line 134 begins a TypeScript test example with describe('AuthService'). No cryptographic algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptive text and a test suite name. There is no hash, cipher, crypto import, or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 31, 32, 98, and 111 discuss test isolation, avoiding external dependencies, anti-patterns, and state edge cases. They do not instruct system discovery or collection of host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is software testing guidance. No commands, filesystem enumeration, process listing, network probing, or environment inspection are requested.

content.value_statement: Teams often start implementation before the expected behavior is clear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create failing tests that define behavior before code changes begin.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of failing tests for new behavior in the TDD red phase.
Prompts for framework-appropriate tests across Jest, pytest, JUnit, Go, and RSpec.
Emphasizes behavior coverage, edge cases, error handling, and contract tests.
Requires failures to come from missing behavior, not setup or syntax errors.
Asks for run instructions, failure verification, coverage areas, and green-phase next steps.
limitations: It does not implement production code for the green phase.
It does not guarantee generated tests compile without project-specific context.
It does not replace manual review of test intent and maintainability.
It should not be used against production systems or live external services.

use_cases.title: Define New Feature Behavior
use_cases.description: Create failing tests that describe expected outcomes before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review Edge Case Coverage
use_cases.description: Expand a planned test suite with boundary values, invalid states, and error paths.

use_cases.title: Standardize TDD Practice
use_cases.description: Give teams a repeatable prompt for red-phase test structure and failure verification.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Red-Phase Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate failing tests for this behavior: [describe behavior]. Use the existing test framework and include run instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Edge Case Expansion
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the main behavior is known but edge cases are incomplete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate failing tests for [feature]. Cover happy path, null or empty values, boundaries, invalid inputs, and expected errors.

prompt_templates.title: Framework-Specific Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the project has a clear testing framework.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate red-phase tests for [feature] using [framework]. Include fixtures, test names, assertions, and why each test should fail now.

prompt_templates.title: Full TDD Red Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before implementation on a complex feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete failing test suite for [feature]. Include unit, integration, contract, and property-style cases where relevant. Verify the failures are caused by missing behavior.

output_examples.input: Generate failing tests for password reset token expiration.
output_examples.output: A test plan with cases for valid tokens, expired tokens, missing tokens, reused tokens, and clear failure reasons before implementation.

output_examples.input: Create red-phase tests for a shopping cart discount rule.
output_examples.output: A framework-specific test suite outline covering qualifying totals, non-qualifying totals, boundary amounts, invalid coupons, and expected assertions.

output_examples.input: Add edge case coverage before building a CSV importer.
output_examples.output: A failing test set covering empty files, malformed rows, Unicode data, duplicate headers, permission errors, and import summary expectations.

best_practices: Provide the target framework, feature behavior, and relevant existing test conventions.
Run generated tests before implementation and confirm they fail for the intended reason.
Keep each generated test focused on one observable behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for implementation code during the red phase.
Do not write tests that depend on production services or live data.
Do not accept tests that pass before the behavior is implemented.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates failing tests and test plans for the TDD red phase.

faq.question: Does it implement the feature?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on defining expected behavior before implementation.

faq.question: Which test frameworks does it mention?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for Jest, Vitest, pytest, JUnit, Go tests, and RSpec.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt guidance is suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Why should the tests fail first?
faq.answer: A failing test confirms the expected behavior is not already present and defines the work for the green phase.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the behavior, framework, existing test style, edge cases, and any interface contracts.
